name: International Stem Cell CORP
business: International Stem Cell CORP is a clinical stage biotechnology company with two revenue-generating businesses: anti-aging skin care products and human cell culture research products. It is also developing stem cell therapies for Parkinson's disease, traumatic brain injury, and stroke.
sellsTo: therapeutic market (Parkinson's disease, traumatic brain injury, stroke)
biomedical research market (pharmaceutical, academic, government researchers)

exposures.sentence: We are developing different cell types from our stem cells that may result in therapeutic products.

ISCO's wholly owned subsidiary Lifeline Skin Care, Inc. (LSC) develops, manufactures and sells anti-aging skin care products based on two core technologies: encapsulated extract derived from hpSC and specially selected targeted small molecules.
ISCO's LCT subsidiary develops, manufactures, and commercializes over 200 human cell culture products.

catalysts.event: Announcement of full Phase 1 clinical trial results for ISC-hpNSC in Parkinson's disease
catalysts.sentence: We expect to announce the full Phase 1 clinical trial results in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Parkinson's disease: more than one million sufferers in the United States with over $2 billion spent on related medication costs
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Parkinson's Disease Foundation, there are more than one million sufferers in the United States with over $2 billion spent on related medication costs.

tamClaims.claim: Traumatic brain injury: over 1.7 million people in North America suffer annually, with associated medical costs exceeding $70 billion; global incidence approximately 10 million people annually
tamClaims.sentence: Over 1.7 million people in North America suffer annually from traumatic brain injury, with associated medical costs exceeding $70 billion.
